channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DOJ Civil Rights Division sends election records request to wrong Michigan office in 2024 probe
item.description: The Department of Justice's Civil Rights Division, led by Assistant Attorney General Harmeet Dhillon, has faced criticism after sending a records demand related to Detroit's 2024 election results to t...

item.title: Comelec trains 9,000 PNP officers to assist in Bangsamoro parliamentary elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has prepared 9,000 personnel from the Philippine National Police (PNP) to serve as standby electoral board members for the first Bangsamoro parliamentary election...

item.title: Union Health Ministry Assesses Ebola Preparedness Across States, No Cases in India
item.description: On Wednesday, Union Health Secretary Punya Salila Srivastava convened a high-level meeting with health secretaries from all states and Union Territories to review India’s preparedness for Ebola Virus ...

item.title: Confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the USA, Canada, and Mexico
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, set to take place in the United States, Canada, and Mexico from 11 June, will feature an expanded 48-team format with each nation naming a 26-man squad. Countries must submit ...

item.title: DNA Confirms 11,000-Year-Old Human Remains in Northern Britain Belonged to a Young Girl
item.description: Archaeologists have identified the oldest known human remains in Northern Britain as those of a young girl who lived approximately 11,000 years ago. The remains were discovered in Heaning Wood Bone Ca...

item.title: Challenges in Teacher Training Affecting Classroom Performance in Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania keeps training many future teachers every year, but a new research from University of Dodoma shows that lots of trainees enter classrooms without enough preparation, support, or guidance. The...

item.title: Pakistan's Interior Minister Naqvi holds high-level talks in Tehran to support US-Iran negotiations
item.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visited Tehran for the second time in less than a week to meet with Iran's top civil and military leaders, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, IRGC Chief General Ahma...

item.title: Matatus Resume Service in Nairobi, Businesses Struggle with Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Nairobi streets woke up to moving matatus on Wednesday morning after a tough two-day strike left the city nearly paralysed. The strike, led by matatu operators protesting high fuel prices, had caused ...

item.title: Ann-Margret: Iconic Actress and Singer Who Defined 1960s Hollywood
item.description: Ann-Margret emerged as one of the most dynamic and talented performers of the 1960s, captivating audiences with her vibrant energy, powerful voice, and magnetic screen presence. Often dubbed the 'fema...

item.title: House approves bill making national election days regular non-working holidays
item.description: The House of Representatives has approved on second reading House Bill No. 8392, which seeks to declare national elections and related electoral activities as regular non-working holidays. The aim is ...

item.title: DA questions IFP MEC over branding of government water tankers in KwaZulu-Natal
item.description: Tensions are rising in KwaZulu-Natal’s coalition government as the Democratic Alliance (DA) has accused Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) Cogta MEC Reverend Thulasizwe Buthelezi of misusing state resources ...

item.title: New Podcast Examines Inter-Agency Rivalry After Major Cocaine Interception in Operation Beech
item.description: A new podcast by The West Australian, titled Behind The White Line and hosted by investigative journalist Richard Baker, explores the dramatic events and consequences of Operation Beech. This sophisti...

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda: Over 500 Suspected Cases and More Than 130 Deaths Reported
item.description: An Ebola virus outbreak involving the rare Bundibugyo strain has been reported in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with the World Health Organization confirming more than 500 suspected cas...

item.title: Political and Social Instability in Britain Amid Cultural and Economic Challenges
item.description: The United Kingdom is currently facing a deep political and social crisis, with recent local elections highlighting challenges to both the Prime Minister and the entrenched ruling elite. British affai...

item.title: Maintenance works begin at Malankara dam, shutters opened to lower water level
item.description: The shutters of the Malankara dam, which is part of the Muvattupuzha Valley Irrigation Project (MVIP) in Idukki, Kerala, have been opened to initiate major repair and maintenance works. According to o...

item.title: Chimoney, Nigerian Fintech, Closes Operations Due to Insufficient Funding
item.description: Chimoney, one fintech wey dey Nigeria but also dey operate from Canada, don close shop after e no fit raise enough money to continue. The company, wey Uchi Uchibeke start for 2022, dey provide cross-b...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney promotes nation-building projects and positive agenda during Vancouver speech amid pipeline discussions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney addressed a business audience at the Greater Vancouver Board of Trade, emphasising a forward-looking nation-building agenda focused on what Canadians support rather than opp...

item.title: Government seeks Sh19.3 billion to tackle worsening drought and food insecurity
item.description: Kenya is facing a worsening drought situation that threatens millions of vulnerable citizens with hunger and humanitarian distress. The State Department for Special Programmes has appealed to Parliame...

item.title: CSA clarifies ticket allocations for Newlands New Year's Test amid fan frustration
item.description: Cricket South Africa (CSA) has come under criticism after tickets for the highly anticipated New Year’s Test between the Proteas and England at Newlands sold out within minutes. Only a small number of...

item.title: Family Calls for Reopened Investigation After Winnipeg Plasma Donor Dies of Heart Condition
item.description: Rodiyat Alabede, a 22-year-old international student, died after donating plasma at a for-profit Grifols centre in Winnipeg. Her family’s representative, Katherine Lanteigne, stated that Alabede had a...
